category_title: Students with high Callous-Unemotional (CU) traits, proactive aggression, and reduced emotional responsiveness
minimal_context: The student is participating in playtime where another child is currently playing with a ball.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for playtime. You can go join the other children.

STUDENT: I want to play with the ball that boy is playing with.

TUTOR: He has the ball right now. What do you think you should do?

STUDENT: Well he was doing that as well.

TUTOR: Just go over there and try to get along.

STUDENT: (Hurts the other child to take the ball) If I hurt him, he will stop playing with the ball, then I can use it to start my own game of football.

options.A: Record the behavioral data in scatter plots and ABC charts when the playtime session finishes.
options.B: Adopt a 'leaky' and 'viscous' orientation to control, rather than instituting an inflexible disciplinary regime.
options.C: Use facilitative strategies and communication temptations with the student during the playtime session.
options.D: Use video work to review the student's behavior and discuss what others think and see.
